categories.name: Software Engineering & Testing
categories.description: These articles serve as my understanding of software testing. They are based on the slides from the software testing lecture when I was a student at LMU.

articles.title: A Guide to Black-Box Testing: Equivalence, Boundary, and Decision Table Techniques
articles.summary: A look at black-box testing, a method where test cases are derived from the external specification without considering the internal code structure.

articles.title: The Test Oracle Problem: How Do You Know Your Test *Really* Passed?
articles.summary: A test input is only half of a test case. The other half is the oracle. This post explores the challenge of determining the correct output for a given test.

articles.title: Measuring Test Suite Quality: A Guide to Coverage Criteria and Mutation Testing
articles.summary: How do you assess the quality of a test suite? This post covers adequacy criteria, from statement and branch coverage to mutation testing.

articles.title: An Introduction to White-Box Testing: Symbolic Execution and Search-Based Testing
articles.summary: An exploration of white-box testing, where the internal structure of the program is used to derive test inputs, focusing on symbolic execution and SBST.

articles.title: Managing Evolving Code: A Guide to Regression Testing
articles.summary: Software is not static. This post covers the techniques for regression testing: minimization, selection, and prioritization.

articles.title: A Look at Concolic Execution: The Hybrid of Concrete and Symbolic Testing
articles.summary: Concolic execution (CONCrete + symbOLIC) is a hybrid approach that addresses the practical limitations of pure symbolic execution.

categories.name: Cybersecurity & Machine Learning
categories.description: Explorations into cybersecurity concepts and machine learning applications.

articles.title: From Random Guesses to Intelligent Grammars: A Look at Fuzzing and Random Testing
articles.summary: A look at random testing and fuzzing, from simple mutational fuzzers to advanced grammar-based fuzzing for generating syntactically valid inputs.
